name: Gurudev Kalicharan Brahma
shortBio: Early Bodo reformer who petitioned for political reservations in 1929.
biography: Gurudev Kalicharan Brahma led early Bodo socio-religious reform and political awakening in the early 1900s. He is noted for submitting a 1929 memorandum to the Simon Commission seeking political reservations and a separate entity for the Bodo people.
contributions: Submitted 1929 memorandum to the Simon Commission for Bodo reservations and a separate entity

timeline.title: Simon Commission memorandum
timeline.description: Submitted a memorandum seeking political reservations and a separate entity for the Bodo people.

name: Joy Bhadra Hagjer
shortBio: Founding president of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha.
biography: Joy Bhadra Hagjer was the founding president of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha, the apex body for Bodo language and literature. His leadership in the mid-1900s helped institutionalize Bodo cultural and literary advancement.
contributions: Founding President of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha

timeline.title: Bodo Sahitya Sabha founded
timeline.description: Led the formation of the apex Bodo language and literature organization.

name: Sonaram Thaosen
shortBio: Founding General Secretary of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha.
biography: Sonaram Thaosen served as the founding General Secretary of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha. His work in the mid-1900s helped organize the Bodo literary movement and institutional leadership.
contributions: Founding General Secretary of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha

name: Bineswar Brahma
shortBio: Literary leader and former BSS president, assassinated in 2000.
biography: Bineswar Brahma was a leading Bodo literary figure and former president of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha. He was assassinated in 2000, marking a major loss for the community's cultural leadership.
contributions: Former President of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha

timeline.title: Assassination
timeline.description: Assassinated in 2000, ending a prominent literary and cultural leadership role.

name: Brajendra Kumar Brahma
shortBio: Former president of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha.
biography: Brajendra Kumar Brahma served as president of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha during the late 1900s and 2000s. His tenure continued the institution's role in Bodo language and cultural preservation.
contributions: Former President of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha

name: Supen Chandra Brahma
shortBio: Current Bodo Sahitya Sabha president (as of 2025).
biography: Supen Chandra Brahma is the current president of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha as of 2025. He leads the apex body for Bodo language and literature in the 2020s.
contributions: President of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha

timeline.title: BSS presidency
timeline.description: Serving as President of the Bodo Sahitya Sabha.

name: Kanakeswar Narzary
shortBio: Founding General Secretary of ABSU.
biography: Kanakeswar Narzary was the founding General Secretary of the All Bodo Students' Union (ABSU). His leadership in the late 1900s helped establish a key platform for Bodo political mobilization.

name: Upendra Nath Brahma
shortBio: Bodofa who launched the separate Bodoland movement in 1987.
biography: Upendra Nath Brahma, known as Bodofa, is revered as the Father of the Bodos. As ABSU President, he launched the movement for a separate Bodoland state in 1987.
contributions: Launched the separate Bodoland state movement in 1987

timeline.title: Bodoland movement launched
timeline.description: As ABSU President, initiated the movement for a separate Bodoland state.

name: Premsing Brahma
shortBio: Founder of BVF and former Chief of the Bodoland Autonomous Council.
biography: Premsing Brahma founded the Bodo Volunteers Force in 1987, the first Bodo militant group. He later served as Chief of the Bodoland Autonomous Council from 1993 to 1996 and became a Congress leader.
contributions: Founded the Bodo Volunteers Force in 1987
Chief of the Bodoland Autonomous Council (1993-1996)

timeline.title: Bodo Volunteers Force founded
timeline.description: Founded the first Bodo militant group.

timeline.title: Headed Bodoland Autonomous Council
timeline.description: Became Chief of the Bodoland Autonomous Council (1993-1996).

name: S.K. Bwiswmuthiary
shortBio: Interim leader of the Bodoland Executive Council after the 1993 Accord.
biography: S.K. Bwiswmuthiary served as the interim leader of the Bodoland Executive Council after the 1993 Bodo Accord. His role helped guide the transition phase in the 1990s.
contributions: Interim leader of the Bodoland Executive Council after the 1993 Accord

timeline.title: Interim BEC leadership
timeline.description: Led the Bodoland Executive Council during the post-accord transition.

name: Pramila Rani Brahma
shortBio: Movement leader who sustained the Bodo struggle through peaceful mobilization.
biography: Pramila Rani Brahma helped sustain the Bodo movement during the 1980s and 1990s through peaceful protests and cultural programs. She is recognized for mobilizing community support in difficult periods.
contributions: Sustained the movement through peaceful protests
Led cultural programs to maintain community momentum

name: Bir Chilagang Basumatary
shortBio: Founding chairman of the Bodo Liberation Tigers in 1996.
biography: Bir Chilagang Basumatary was the founding chairman of the Bodo Liberation Tigers (BLT) in 1996. His leadership marked a major militant phase in the Bodo movement during the late 1990s and 2000s.
contributions: Founding Chairman of the Bodo Liberation Tigers in 1996

timeline.title: BLT founded
timeline.description: Became founding chairman of the Bodo Liberation Tigers.

name: Charan Narzary
shortBio: Founder General Secretary of PTCA and former MLA/MP.
biography: Charan Narzary was the founding General Secretary of the Plains Tribal Council of Assam (PTCA). He served as both an MLA and an MP, representing Bodo interests from the 1970s through the 2010s.

name: Samar Brahma Chowdhury
shortBio: Senior Bodo leader and Janata Dal minister in Assam.
biography: Samar Brahma Chowdhury emerged as one of the main Bodo leaders in the 1970s. He later became a minister in the first Janata Dal cabinet in Assam during the 1980s.
contributions: Minister in the first Janata Dal cabinet in Assam

name: Hagrama Mohilary
shortBio: Former BLT chief and first BTC Chief Executive Member.
biography: Hagrama Mohilary, a former BLT chief, became the first Chief Executive Member of the Bodoland Territorial Council in 2005. He led the BTC until 2020 and chairs the Bodoland People's Front.

timeline.title: First BTC CEM
timeline.description: Became the first Chief Executive Member of the Bodoland Territorial Council.

name: Pramod Boro
shortBio: ABSU president and signatory of the 2020 Bodo Accord.
biography: Pramod Boro, President of ABSU, emerged as a key rival to Hagrama Mohilary and later became CEM of the BTR. He was a signatory to the 2020 Bodo Accord.

timeline.title: 2020 Bodo Accord
timeline.description: Signed the Bodo Accord and rose to leadership in the BTR.

name: Rihon Daimary
shortBio: Deputy Chief Executive Member of the BTC.
biography: Rihon Daimary serves as Deputy Chief Executive Member of the BTC and is also a former MLA. His leadership is associated with the current BTR administration in the 2020s.
contributions: Deputy Chief Executive Member of BTC

name: Tridip Daimary
shortBio: Speaker of the BTC Legislative Assembly.
biography: Tridip Daimary serves as Speaker of the BTC Legislative Assembly in the 2020s. He plays a central role in the legislative functioning of the council.
contributions: Speaker of the BTC Legislative Assembly

name: Lawrence Islary
shortBio: UPPL MLA active in BTR-era politics.
biography: Lawrence Islary is an MLA from the United People's Party Liberal (UPPL). He represents BTR-era political leadership in the 2020s.

name: Charan Boro
shortBio: Two-time Majbat MLA and Assam cabinet minister (2025).
biography: Charan Boro is a two-time Majbat MLA and was sworn in as an Assam cabinet minister in October 2025 as part of a BPF-BJP alliance. He represents contemporary Bodo political leadership at the state level.

timeline.title: Assam cabinet minister
timeline.description: Sworn in as a minister in the Assam cabinet.

name: Durga Das Boro
shortBio: Former BPF MLA in the 2020s.
biography: Durga Das Boro is a former BPF MLA associated with Bodo regional politics in the 2020s. His role reflects party-level leadership within the BTC and BTR context.

name: Rwngwra Narzary
shortBio: Former ABSU president in the 2000s and 2010s.
biography: Rwngwra Narzary served as President of ABSU in the 2000s and 2010s. He helped guide student-led political mobilization during a key transitional period.

name: Urkhao Gwra Brahma
shortBio: Former Rajya Sabha MP representing Bodo leadership nationally.
biography: Urkhao Gwra Brahma (UG Brahma) served as a Rajya Sabha MP in the 2010s and 2020s. He represents Bodo political leadership at the national parliamentary level.

name: Jwhwlao Swmbla Basumatary
shortBio: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery.
biography: Jwhwlao Swmbla Basumatary is honored with a bronze statue at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery. The recognition reflects a symbolic role in the Bodo movement.
contributions: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery

name: Subungthini Thandwi
shortBio: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery.
biography: Subungthini Thandwi is commemorated with a bronze statue at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery. The honor reflects legacy and sacrifice in the Bodo movement.
contributions: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery

name: Anthai Gwra Bodosa
shortBio: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery.
biography: Anthai Gwra Bodosa is honored with a bronze statue at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery. The commemoration recognizes contributions to the Bodo movement.
contributions: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery

name: Horkhab Narzary
shortBio: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery.
biography: Horkhab Narzary is commemorated with a bronze statue at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery. The honor reflects recognized sacrifice within the Bodo movement.
contributions: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery

name: Baliram Boro
shortBio: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery.
biography: Baliram Boro is honored with a bronze statue at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery. The recognition underscores his symbolic place in the Bodo movement.
contributions: Honored at the Bodoland Martyrs' Cemetery

name: Kampha Borgoyari
shortBio: Deputy to Hagrama Mohilary within the BPF.
biography: Kampha Borgoyari served as a deputy to Hagrama Mohilary within the BPF leadership structure. His role reflects party-level influence in BTC-era governance.
contributions: Deputy to Hagrama Mohilary in BPF

name: Chandan Brahma
shortBio: Assam Transport Minister under Congress.
biography: Chandan Brahma served as Assam Transport Minister under the Congress. His role represents Bodo participation in state-level governance.

name: Rabiram Narzary
shortBio: BPPF president and later BPF leader and MCLA.
biography: Rabiram Narzary served as BPPF President and later became a BPF leader and MCLA. His work spans party leadership and council-level representation.

name: Jebaram Mushahary
shortBio: Leader of PJACBM.
biography: Jebaram Mushahary led the PJACBM, a key organization in Bodo political advocacy. His leadership reflects civil mobilization within the movement.

name: Dhiren Boro
shortBio: Chairman of the NDFB(P) faction.
biography: Dhiren Boro serves as Chairman of the NDFB(P). His position reflects leadership within a faction of the NDFB in the contemporary period.

name: Gobinda Basumatary
shortBio: Secretary of the NDFB(P) faction.
biography: Gobinda Basumatary served as secretary of NDFB(P). His role is associated with organizational leadership within the faction.

name: Mritunjoy Narzary
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Mritunjoy Narzary served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC. His role reflects BPF leadership in BTC governance.

name: Munmun Brahma
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC and early woman leader.
biography: Munmun (Moon Moon) Brahma served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC and was among the first two women in the BTC Executive Council. Her role marked an early step in expanding women's representation.
contributions: One of the first two women in the BTC Executive Council

name: Derhasat Basumatary
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Derhasat Basumatary served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC under the BPF leadership. His role reflects council-level governance responsibilities.

name: Prakash Basumatary
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Prakash Basumatary served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC under the BPF. He contributed to council administration during that term.

name: Paniram Brahma
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Paniram Brahma served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC for the BPF. His role was part of BTC governance in that term.

name: Dhiraj Borgoyari
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Dhiraj Borgoyari served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC under the BPF. His role reflects administrative leadership within BTC governance.

name: Begum Akhtara Ahmed
shortBio: First Muslim woman elected to the Bodoland Council.
biography: Begum Akhtara Ahmed served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC and became the first Muslim woman elected to the Bodoland Council. Her role reflects expanded representation in BTC governance.
contributions: First Muslim woman elected to the Bodoland Council

name: Agustis Tigga
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Agustis (Augustush) Tigga served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC under the BPF. His role contributed to council administration.

name: Ganesh Kachari
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Ganesh Kachari (Kochary) served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC under the BPF. He held council responsibilities during that term.

name: Loumshrao Daimary
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Loumshrao (Lwmsaro) Daimary served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC under the BPF. He contributed to BTC administration during that term.

name: Fresh Mushahary
shortBio: Executive Member in the 5th BTC.
biography: Fresh Mushahary served as an Executive Member in the 5th BTC under the BPF. His role reflects leadership in BTC governance.

name: Sabharam Basumatary
shortBio: BPF candidate for Gossaigaon in 2026 Assembly elections.
biography: Sabharam Basumatary is a BPF candidate for the Gossaigaon constituency in the 2026 Assembly elections. His candidacy reflects ongoing party leadership in the region.

timeline.description: Contested as BPF candidate for Gossaigaon constituency.

biography: Rupam Roy is a BPF candidate for the Baokhungri constituency in the 2026 Assembly elections. His candidacy reflects party participation in BTR-era electoral politics.

timeline.title: Assembly election candidate
timeline.description: Contested as BPF candidate for Baokhungri constituency.

shortBio: BPF candidate for Parbotjhora in 2026 Assembly elections.
biography: Dr. Rezul Karim is a BPF candidate for the Parbotjhora constituency in the 2026 Assembly elections. His candidacy reflects BPF's cross-community political presence.

timeline.description: Contested as BPF candidate for Parbotjhora constituency.

name: Thaneswar Basumatary
shortBio: BPF candidate for Manas in 2026 Assembly elections.
biography: Thaneswar Basumatary is a BPF candidate for the Manas constituency in the 2026 Assembly elections. His candidacy reflects party leadership at the constituency level.

timeline.title: Assembly election candidate
timeline.description: Contested as BPF candidate for Manas constituency.

name: Maneswar Brahma
shortBio: BPF candidate for Baksa in 2026 Assembly elections.
biography: Maneswar Brahma is a BPF candidate for the Baksa constituency in the 2026 Assembly elections. His candidacy highlights ongoing BPF electoral participation.

timeline.title: Assembly election candidate
timeline.description: Contested as BPF candidate for Baksa constituency.

name: Maheswar Boro
shortBio: BPF candidate for Bhergaon in 2026 Assembly elections.
biography: Maheswar Boro is a BPF candidate for the Bhergaon constituency in the 2026 Assembly elections. His candidacy represents BPF's constituency-level strategy.

timeline.title: Assembly election candidate
timeline.description: Contested as BPF candidate for Bhergaon constituency.

name: B. Saoraigwra
shortBio: Chairman of an NDFB faction.
biography: B. Saoraigwra served as chairman of an NDFB faction. His role is associated with militant leadership within the broader Bodo movement.

name: IK Sangbijit
shortBio: Founder of the NDFB(S) faction.
biography: IK Sangbijit founded the NDFB(S) faction. His leadership marks a distinct militant faction within the Bodo movement.
